CheckTitle: Conditional Access policy requires MDM-compliant device for all cloud app access
Description: A Conditional Access policy enforces **MDM-compliant device** as a mandatory requirement for accessing all cloud applications. This ensures only devices enrolled in and compliant with Intune MDM policies can authenticate to cloud resources.
Risk: Without mandatory device compliance, users can access cloud resources from **unmanaged or non-compliant devices** that may lack encryption, antivirus, or OS updates. This increases the risk of **data exfiltration** and **credential theft** from compromised endpoints.

Code.Other:
1. Navigate to the Microsoft Entra admin center https://entra.microsoft.com.
2. Expand **Protection** > **Conditional Access** and select **Policies**.
3. Select **New policy**.
4. Under **Users**, select **All users** and configure break-glass account exclusions.
5. Under **Target resources**, select **All cloud apps**.
6. Under **Grant**, select **Grant access** and check **Require device to be marked as compliant**.
7. Ensure the grant operator does not combine this with MFA as an OR alternative.
8. Set the policy to **On** and click **Create**.

Recommendation.Text: Enforce a Conditional Access policy requiring all devices to be **MDM-enrolled and compliant** before accessing cloud applications. Ensure Intune compliance policies are configured and assigned before enabling this policy. Exclude break-glass accounts to prevent lockout scenarios.

Notes: Intune enrollment is automatically excluded from this policy to avoid a chicken-and-egg problem. Compliance policies must be created and assigned in Intune before enabling this Conditional Access policy.
